content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Riding in a Lift". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/479/problem/E
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of people. Then n lines follow, each containing two integers: the floor the person is currently on and the floor they want to go to.
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of times the lift needs to stop.

solution.approach: Sort the people based on their current floor and then their destination floor. Initialize two pointers, one at the beginning and one at the end of the sorted list. Move the pointers towards each other, counting the number of stops.

code.Python3:
import sys
n = int(sys.stdin.readline())
people = [list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())) for _ in range(n)]
people.sort(key=lambda x: (x[0], x[1]))
stops = 0
i, j = 0, n - 1
while i <= j:
    if people[i][0] < people[j][0]:
        stops += 1
        i += 1
    elif people[i][0] > people[j][0]:
        stops += 1
        j -= 1
    else:
        stops += 1
        i += 1
        j -= 1
print(stops)
